[198:41]So, we are in a situation where, as we've said repeatedly throughout the past few weeks,

[198:47]Trump was not able to achieve his objectives.

[198:51]Iran survived the killing blow and now they're not going anywhere, because the attack failed,

[198:58]it has actually strengthened and buttressed the regime.

[199:01]And so we failed, they succeeded.

[199:04]And now Iran is inflicting the maximum amount of pain to deter future aggression, another

[199:10]attempt to topple their government.

[199:13]And not only that, but they have also taken control of the Strait of Hormuz in such a

[199:19]It might be impossible for us to undo this situation.

[199:23]And if we don't fix it now, we may never fix it because that would entail repeating

[199:28]this war, which would entail all the same problems that we're encountering now, a shortage

[199:34]of weapons, a shortage of munitions, the inability to suppress Iran's tactical capability to

[199:42]launch and make drones and ballistic missiles.

[199:45]So, this is truly a dire situation.

[199:47]And as we've said, there's really just two options, Trump can either escalate or de-escalate.

[199:54]He can either ramp up the tactics and the only way you can do that is with ground forces.

[200:00]We are doing everything that we are capable of in the air and from the sea.

[200:04]And so the only domain that we can actually even extend the fighting into is with some

[200:09]kind of ground presence.

[200:12]Whether that's an amphibious invasion,

[200:15]whether that's using a proxy force like the Kurds,

[200:18]whether we're backing an anti-government group

[200:20]on some timeline, that's the only way

[200:24]that we can escalate from a modal point of view,

[200:26]escalating the mode of fighting

[200:28]to achieve the initial objectives

[200:31]which are regime change or disabling every position

[200:35]where they've hidden and are able

[200:36]to launch drones and missiles.

[200:42]And we beg Iran for a truce through intermediaries like through Pakistan and China.

[200:48]And that's it.

[200:50]Those are your options.

[200:57]exit.

[200:58]And that has been the story for the past few weeks.

[201:09]Either of the islands or of the mainland,

[201:12]an attempt to escalate in order to save face, gain leverage,

[201:17]dictate terms to end the conflict,

[201:20]or is Trump effectively just going to bow out and surrender?

[201:29]it's unlikely that Trump will surrender.

[201:35]based on what we know about the pressures in the region,

[201:38]our allies like Saudi Arabia and the Emirates and Israel,

[201:43]they want us to keep going

[201:44]and they're pressuring us to keep going.

[201:46]Based on the conditions in this conflict,

[201:49]if we wrap it up now,

[201:51]almost certainly this is going to become a problem again

[201:54]in the future.

[201:55]The leadership that we killed has been replaced

[201:58]by hardliners that are less willing to negotiate

[202:01]and more extreme.

[202:03]Because of the way we conducted ourselves in the conflict,

[202:07]we have probably alienated the Iranian people

[202:09]and made an uprising less likely, not more likely.

[202:19]for the economy at a global level

[202:21]that if we even were to consider going back in

[202:24]and finishing the job,

[202:33]entire world is going to protest a second go at it, a second attempt to try to do this

[202:40]all over again.

[202:41]And almost certainly that is what the situation would call for on a long enough timeline.

[202:53]Engaging to retake and rebuild its missile program, its highly enriched uranium, begin

[202:58]enriching uranium again, you're going to have pressure to, to go back in.

[203:09]can't.

[203:11]There's no way from a strategic point of view, it just isn't sound for us to just wash our

[203:25]They're going to dictate the timeline and the terms.

[204:11]is how I would describe it.

[204:19]across the board.

[204:21]There is a messaging blitz, which seems to want all of us to believe that this war is

[204:27]going to end.

[204:29]And this is what I hear everybody saying in the market seem to believe it as well.

[204:57]going to be okay.

[211:06]of what Trump demanded.

Topic 6: Part 4 (the bluff, isolation & )

[224:31]It's April Fools. I think that Trump is going to go out there. He's going to tout all of

[226:37]because they're going to hold the straight hostage with drones and missiles. Well, try
